The Senate reconciliation bill, known as the One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA), was passed by the House on May 22, 2025, and signed by President Trump on July 4, 2025. This bill extends the 2017 TCJA individual tax cuts using a 'current policy' baseline in the Senate's budget reconciliation process.

The One Big Beautiful Bill Act (OBBBA) signed into law by President Trump on July 4, 2025, modifies the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) to make domestic content requirements mandatory for obtaining the Production Tax Credits (45Y) for projects beginning construction on or after June 16, 2025.

The domestic content requirements for obtaining the IRA's Investment Tax Credits (48Ε) became mandatory in 2025 due to legislative changes. The Senate Finance Committee's reconciliation bill, released on June 16, 2025, updated the Section 48E domestic content rule for bonus credit eligibility to match Section 45Y. The required domestic content percentages are specified for projects beginning construction before or after certain dates, indicating that compliance with these requirements is mandatory for full Investment Tax Credit eligibility.

The House Reconciliation Bill, H.R. 1, passed on May 22, 2025, amends the Inflation Reduction Act (IRA) and introduces new requirements for the Advanced Manufacturing Production Tax Credits (45X). The bill applies Foreign Entity of Concern (FEOC) restrictions, which include requirements for domestically-produced materials or subcomponents. These restrictions disallow credits to taxpayers who are specified foreign entities or foreign-influenced entities and for projects receiving material assistance from prohibited foreign entities after December 31, 2025.
